_note: Feature tables for the post-apocalyptic classes. n = feature name, d = one-line rules text the DM treats as authoritative.

1.d: 1/short rest, search any ruin or wreck for 10 minutes and find one genuinely useful item (the DM decides what plausibly exists).
Improvised weapons count as proficient simple weapons dealing 1d6, or 1d8 two-handed.

2.d: Carry capacity doubles; you always have basic supplies (rope, tape, wire, a light source).

5.d: Attack twice when taking the Attack action.

6.d: Advantage to spot traps, ambushes, and unstable ground; +2 initiative in ruins.

7.d: 1/rest, repair or rig a device, vehicle, or weapon in 1 minute; it works for a scene.

9.d: Advantage on saves against radiation, poison, and disease.

10.d: 1/long rest, produce exactly the mundane item the party needs; you scavenged it earlier.

11.d: Your improvised and simple weapons deal +1d6 damage and count as magical.

13.d: Disengage or Hide as a bonus action; escape grapples and restraints with advantage.

14.d: Scrap Sense has 2 uses per short rest and takes only 1 minute.

15.d: Your rigged devices from Make It Work last a day and can be handed to allies.

17.d: 1/long rest, your search turns up something rare: old-world tech, ammo cache, or medicine (DM picks the form).

18.d: When disarmed or emptied, you always find another weapon within reach this turn.

20.d: Scrap Sense always finds two items; 1/day one of them is exactly what you ask for.

3.d: A glance tells you what a ruin held, when it fell, and what has moved through since.
Advantage on Stealth while in ruins or wreckage.

7.d: 1/turn, your improvised weapons deal +1d4 poison and the target cannot regain HP until your next turn.

11.d: 1/rest, trigger or predict a structural collapse: creatures in a 15-ft cube save (DEX) or take 4d6 bludgeoning and are restrained by rubble.

15.d: In ruins you cannot be surprised, tracked, or flanked, and your Scrap Sense recharges on any rest.

1.d: You may attack recklessly: advantage on melee attacks this turn, but attacks against you have advantage until your next turn.
1/rest, reduce all damage you take this round by your CON modifier.

2.d: Advantage on checks to drive, ride, or repair vehicles; attacking from a moving vehicle carries no penalty.

5.d: Attack twice when taking the Attack action.

6.d: Advantage on saves against fear; allies within 10 ft add +1d4 to fear saves while you fight on.

7.d: When you move 20+ ft straight toward a target and hit with a melee attack, deal +2d6 and shove it 10 ft.

9.d: Resistance to bludgeoning damage from nonmagical attacks.

10.d: Hardened has 2 uses and reduces damage by twice your CON modifier.

11.d: Attack three times when taking the Attack action.

13.d: While below half HP, your melee attacks deal +1d8 damage.

14.d: 1/rest, when an ally within 30 ft drops to 0 HP, immediately move your speed and make one attack.

15.d: Your speed cannot be reduced and you cannot be knocked prone while conscious.

17.d: 1/long rest, when you drop to 0 HP, keep fighting: you remain conscious at 0 for 1 minute, then fall.

18.d: Allies within 30 ft gain +1 to attack rolls while they can see you.

20.d: Death or Glory no longer grants enemies advantage against you.

3.d: A hooked chain (1d8 piercing, 15-ft reach): on hit, drag the target 10 ft toward you.
Advantage on Intimidation against travelers, traders, and rival crews.

7.d: When you drop a creature, enemies within 15 ft save (WIS) or are frightened of you until your next turn.

11.d: After you hit with Ram Speed, make one extra attack against a different creature within reach.

15.d: 1/long rest, for 1 minute your speed doubles, Ram Speed deals +4d6, and you resist all damage.

1.d: Casts sorcerer-list spells as mutations using CHA; casting visibly warps your flesh for a moment.
While unarmored, AC equals 13 + DEX modifier.

2.d: 1/long rest, after taking elemental damage, gain resistance to that type until you rest.

3.d: Darkvision 60 ft; advantage on Perception checks using smell.

5.d: 2/rest, cast a cantrip as a bonus action after casting a leveled mutation.

7.d: Spend a hit die as a bonus action to heal, CHA-mod uses per long rest.

9.d: Immune to poison and disease; a creature that hits you with a bite takes 2d6 poison.

11.d: Advantage on Stealth; 1/rest, turn near-invisible until you attack or cast.

13.d: Learn two spells from any spell list; they count as mutations for you.

15.d: 1/long rest, regain expended slot levels equal to your CHA modifier when you drop below half HP.

17.d: Resistance to two damage types of your choice, chosen each dawn.

18.d: Your mutations ignore resistance to their damage type.

20.d: 1/long rest, for 1 minute your mutations of 3rd level or lower cost no slots and your Adaptive Hide grants +2 more AC.

1.d: A claw, tendril, or bone blade: a natural weapon dealing 1d8 + CHA slashing, and you can cast mutations through it.

6.d: Rapid Adaptation has 2 uses and can also grant a climb or swim speed for an hour.

10.d: 1/long rest, reaction when hit: split into a swarm shape until your next turn, taking half damage from the attack and moving through gaps freely.

14.d: Your Chimeric Limb deals 2d8, counts as magical, and grants 10-ft reach.

1.d: You can operate, unlock, and read any pre-Fall technology given a minute of fiddling.
2/rest, whack a machine or construct: restore 1d8 + INT HP to it or clear one malfunction.

2.d: Casts wizard-list spells as reclaimed-tech effects using INT; your rebuilt rig is the focus.

5.d: 1/rest, cast a 1st-level tech effect without expending a slot.

6.d: 1/rest, constructs and machines within 15 ft save (CON) or are stunned until your next turn.

9.d: Detect and trace active tech, signals, and power sources within 1 mile.

11.d: During a long rest, build one temporary device that duplicates a 2nd-level tech effect; anyone can trigger it.

13.d: 1/long rest, project a 10-ft energy dome for 1 minute: allies inside gain +2 AC and resistance to ranged weapon damage.

14.d: Power Cell Cache works for tech effects up to 3rd level.

15.d: Constructs and automated defenses must save (WIS) to target you over other creatures.

17.d: Choose one 5th-level tech effect; cast it 1/long rest without a slot.

18.d: 1/long rest, ask the DM one question answerable from pre-Fall records; you recall the answer.

20.d: 1/long rest, for 1 minute your tech effects of 4th level or lower cost no slots; you glow visibly.

3.d: 1/turn, a damaging tech effect deals +1d6 radiant as the core flares.
You sense radiation precisely and are resistant to its effects.

7.d: 1/rest, emit a 10-ft flare: enemies save (CON) or are blinded until your next turn.

11.d: Immunity to radiant and necrotic damage from tech and radiation sources.

15.d: 1/long rest, unleash the core: a 30-ft radius burst dealing 8d6 radiant (CON save halves); allies are spared.

1.d: Casts cleric-list spells as sermons using WIS; your voice carries conviction, not liturgy.
Bonus action: one ally within 30 ft gains temp HP equal to your level + WIS mod, WIS-mod uses per long rest.

2.d: 1/rest, an audible sermon gives allies within 30 ft advantage on their next save against fear or despair.

3.d: 1/day, find or purify enough water and food for six people.

5.d: Your healing sermons add your WIS modifier again to HP restored.

7.d: Advantage on Intimidation and Persuasion against raiders, cultists, and the desperate.

9.d: 1/rest, end one fear, charm, or curse effect on a creature within 30 ft.

11.d: 1/turn, a damaging sermon deals +1d8 radiant to one target.

13.d: Flock's Shepherd can target two allies at once.

15.d: You and allies within 10 ft have resistance to necrotic damage and advantage against fear.

17.d: 1/long rest, receive a true omen about a planned course of action (weal, woe, or both).

18.d: Concentration on your sermons cannot be broken by damage.

20.d: 1/long rest, deliver the full sermon: allies within 60 ft regain 5d8 HP and are freed of fear, charm, and exhaustion by one level.

1.d: 1/rest, your raised hand sheds sunlight in a 30-ft radius for 10 minutes; undead and mutants in it have disadvantage on attacks.

6.d: When you heal a creature below half HP, it also gains +1 to attack rolls until your next turn.

10.d: 1/long rest, when an ally within 30 ft drops to 0 HP, they instead drop to WIS-mod x 5 HP.

14.d: Your Dawnlight damages: hostile undead and aberrations take 2d8 radiant entering or starting their turn in it.

1.d: A loyal wasteland beast fights beside you (AC 13 + your proficiency, HP 5 x level, attack 1d8 + WIS, obeys your commands).
You and your beast have advantage on attacks against a creature the other is adjacent to.

2.d: Casts druid-list spells as old bonds using WIS; you can always communicate simple ideas with beasts.

5.d: Attack twice when taking the Attack action; your beast attacks when you command it as a bonus action.

6.d: You and your beast track with advantage and cannot be surprised by beasts or mutants.

7.d: Your healing bonds may target your beast at double effect.

9.d: 1/long rest, summon 1d4 local beasts to aid you for 10 minutes.

10.d: 1/rest, hostile beasts and mutants within 30 ft save (WIS) or cower (no move toward you) for 1 minute.

11.d: Your beast's attacks deal 2d8 + WIS, count as magical, and it gains +2 AC.

13.d: Bond a second, smaller beast (scout stats); see through either beast's senses within 1 mile.

14.d: When your beast would drop to 0 HP, 1/long rest it drops to 1 instead; the same holds for you while it lives.

15.d: When you and your beast hit the same target in a turn, it saves (STR) or is knocked prone and grappled.

17.d: Beasts and plants will not harm you unless magically forced; advantage on all WIS checks involving them.

18.d: Call the Wild summons 2d4 beasts and recharges on a short rest 1/day.

20.d: While within 30 ft of your beast, you both resist all damage from creatures neither of you has yet wounded this fight.

3.d: 1/rest, you and your beast each gain +2 to damage rolls for 1 minute.
Your beast can guard a person or place independently and alert you at any distance within a mile.

7.d: Reaction: when your beast within 30 ft takes damage, split it evenly between you.

11.d: Your Pack Tactics Training advantage extends to all allies adjacent to your beast.

15.d: 1/long rest, your beast grows monstrous for 1 minute: double HP, +10 ft speed, attacks deal +2d8 and frighten (WIS save).
